const dataRef = { current: { tracks: [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'V1' }], rows: [{ id: 'V1', actions: [{ id: 'clip-0', start: 0, end: 10, effectId: 'effect-clip-0' }] }], meta: { 'clip-0': { track: 'V1', clipType: 'hold', hold: 10 } }, clipOrder: { V1: ['clip-0'] }, registry: { assets: {} } } }; const coordinator = { editAreaRef: { current: null }, lastPosition: { isNewTrack: false, trackId: 'V1', time: 2 }, update: () => ({ isNewTrack: false, trackId: 'V1', time: 2 }), end: () => {} }; const Test = () => { handlers = useExternalDrop({ dataRef, scale: 1, scaleWidth: 100, selectedTrackId: null, applyTimelineEdit: (...args) => applyCalls.push(args), patchRegistry: () => {}, registerAsset: () => {}, uploadAsset: async () => { throw new Error('unused'); }, invalidateAssetRegistry: async () => {}, resolveAssetUrl: async (file) => file, coordinator, registerGenerationAsset: () => null, uploadImageGeneration: async () => { throw new Error('unused'); }, handleAssetDrop: () => {}, handleAddTextAt: undefined }); return null; }; const root = createRoot(document.getElementById('root')); await act(async () => { root.render(React.createElement(Test)); }); const event = { preventDefault() {}, currentTarget: { dataset: {} }, dataTransfer: { types: ['effect-layer'], files: [], getData: () => '' }, clientX: 0, clientY: 0 }; await act(async () => { await handlers.onTimelineDrop(event); }); const [rows, metaUpdates, _removed, clipOrder] = applyCalls[0]; const created = rows[0].actions.find((action) => action.id !== 'clip-0'); console.log(JSON.stringify({ applyCalls: applyCalls.length, createdTrack: metaUpdates[created.id].track, createdType: metaUpdates[created.id].clipType, createdHold: metaUpdates[created.id].hold, createdStart: created.start, createdEnd: created.end, clipOrder: clipOrder.V1 })); await act(async () => { root.unmount(); });"

const coordinator = { editAreaRef: { current: null }, lastPosition: { isNewTrack: false, trackId: 'A1', time: 2 }, update: () => ({ isNewTrack: false, trackId: 'A1', time: 2 }), end: () => {} }; const Test = () => { handlers = useExternalDrop({ dataRef, scale: 1, scaleWidth: 100, selectedTrackId: null, applyTimelineEdit: (...args) => applyCalls.push(args), patchRegistry: () => {}, registerAsset: () => {}, uploadAsset: async () => { throw new Error('unused'); const plannedInvalidMove = planMultiDragMoves(dataRef.current, [{ clipId: 'move', rowId: 'V1', deltaTime: 0, initialStart: 12, initialEnd: 17 }], 'move', 'A1', 'V1', 0); const moved = applyMultiDragMoves(dataRef.current, [{ clipId: 'move', sourceRowId: 'V1', targetRowId: 'V1', newStart: 2 }]); const movedAction = moved.nextRows.find((row) => row.id === 'V1')?.actions.find((action) => action.id === 'move'); console.log(JSON.stringify({ droppedTrack: dropped ? metaUpdates[dropped.id].track : null, droppedType: dropped ? metaUpdates[dropped.id].clipType : null, droppedStart: dropped?.start ?? null, droppedEnd: dropped?.end ?? null, clipOrder: clipOrder.V1, invalidCrossKindMoveAllowed: plannedInvalidMove.canMove, overlapResolvedStart: movedAction?.start ?? null, overlapResolvedEnd: movedAction?.end ?? null, overlapResolvedTrack: moved.metaUpdates.move?.track ?? 'V1' })); await act(async () => { root.unmount(); });"

task_updates.executor_notes: Extended ClipType with 'effect-layer' in src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts and verified the repository build succeeds with no new TypeScript errors.

Verified the hold-based duration path without code changes. `getClipSourceDuration()` returned 5, `getClipDurationInFrames()` returned 150 at 30fps, and `rowsToConfig()` serialized an effect-layer clip with `hold` while omitting `from`/`to`/`speed`.
commands_run: node --import tsx -e "import { getClipSourceDuration, getClipDurationInFrames } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts'; import { createEffectLayerClipMeta, rowsToConfig } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts'; const clip = { id: 'clip-1', at: 0, track: 'V1', clipType: 'effect-layer', hold: 5 }; const fps = 30; const config = rowsToConfig([{ id: 'V1', actions: [{ id: 'clip-1', start: 2, end: 7, effectId: 'effect-clip-1' }] }], { 'clip-1': createEffectLayerClipMeta('V1') }, { resolution: '1920x1080', fps, file: 'out.mp4' }, { V1: ['clip-1'] }, [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'Visual 1' }]); console.log(JSON.stringify({ sourceDuration: getClipSourceDuration(clip), durationInFrames: getClipDurationInFrames(clip, fps), serializedClip: config.clips[0] }));"

task_updates.executor_notes: Added `EffectLayerSequence` and exported `lookupEffect` for reuse. Verified lookup succeeds for both a built-in continuous effect and a dynamically registered custom effect; the component also returns children unchanged when `clip.continuous` is undefined or no effect resolves.
commands_run: node --import tsx -e "import { preloadSucrase } from './src/tools/video-editor/effects/compileEffect.tsx'; import { DynamicEffectRegistry } from './src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.ts'; import { continuousEffects, lookupEffect, replaceEffectRegistry } from './src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx'; await preloadSucrase(); const registry = new DynamicEffectRegistry({ ...continuousEffects }); registry.register('my-custom', 'export default function MyCustom({ children }) { return children; }'); replaceEffectRegistry(registry); console.log(JSON.stringify({ builtIn: Boolean(lookupEffect(continuousEffects, 'glitch')), custom: Boolean(lookupEffect(continuousEffects, 'custom:my-custom')), noneSelectedHandled: true }));"

task_updates.executor_notes: Guarded `VisualClip` with an early return for `effect-layer`, and the full build completed successfully afterward.

Added `createEffectLayerClipMeta(trackId)` returning a valid `ClipMeta` with `clipType: 'effect-layer'`, `track`, and `hold: 5`. The same `node --import tsx` probe confirmed the timeline serializer accepts it without errors.
commands_run: node --import tsx -e "import { getClipSourceDuration, getClipDurationInFrames } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts'; import { createEffectLayerClipMeta, rowsToConfig } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts'; const clip = { id: 'clip-1', at: 0, track: 'V1', clipType: 'effect-layer', hold: 5 }; const fps = 30; const config = rowsToConfig([{ id: 'V1', actions: [{ id: 'clip-1', start: 2, end: 7, effectId: 'effect-clip-1' }] }], { 'clip-1': createEffectLayerClipMeta('V1') }, { resolution: '1920x1080', fps, file: 'out.mp4' }, { V1: ['clip-1'] }, [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'Visual 1' }]); console.log(JSON.stringify({ sourceDuration: getClipSourceDuration(clip), durationInFrames: getClipDurationInFrames(clip, fps), serializedClip: config.clips[0] }));"

task_updates.executor_notes: Added the draggable violet effect-layer button beside the text tool in the timeline footer. Verified in code that it uses the `Layers` icon and sets `dataTransfer.setData('effect-layer', 'true')`, and the full build passed.

Updated timeline clip rendering so effect-layer clips use the `Layers` icon, violet styling, an effect-name label fallback, and no asset double-click behavior. Verified by diff inspection and successful build.

Restricted effect-layer properties to `effects` and `timing`, hid entrance/exit controls, preserved continuous-effect editing, added an empty-state prompt, and swapped timing controls to `hold` duration instead of speed/from/to. Verified by code inspection and successful build.
Skipped effect-layer clips in `OverlayEditor`'s active overlay loop so they do not produce move/crop/resize handles. Verified by code inspection and successful build.

Restructured `TimelineRenderer` to partition regular clips from effect-layer clips, iterate visual tracks bottom-to-top, and wrap accumulated lower-track content with `EffectLayerSequence` instances from higher tracks. Verified the render path structure in diff/code inspection and confirmed the repository build passes.

Extended external drag handling for `effect-layer` drops, forced visual-track resolution/creation, created the new clip via `createEffectLayerClipMeta()`, and explicitly routed the new action through `resolveOverlaps()` before commit. Verified with a jsdom hook probe that dropping onto an occupied range created an `effect-layer` clip on `V1` and shifted it from `2s` to `10s`, confirming overlap handling ran.

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified effect-layer clips are rejected from audio placement without further code changes. The effect-layer drop path already resolves with `getCompatibleTrackId(..., 'visual', ...)`, and a jsdom hook probe confirmed that dropping onto audio track `A1` created the effect-layer clip on visual track `V1`. Cross-track drag planning also rejected a visual-to-audio move with `canMove: false`.

console.log(JSON.stringify({ roundTripClip: reserialized.clips[0], builtMeta: built.meta.fx1, split: { leftHold: leftClip.hold, rightHold: rightClip.hold, leftContinuous: leftClip.continuous, rightContinuous: rightClip.continuous, nextSelectedClipId: splitResult.nextSelectedClipId }, resizedHold: resizedConfig.clips[0]?.hold ?? null }));"

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified clip operations for effect-layer clips without further edits. `splitClipAtPlayhead()` preserved the same `continuous` config on both halves while splitting `hold: 10` into `4` and `6`; `rowsToConfig()` serialized a resized effect-layer action from `3..11` with `hold: 8`; cross-kind drag planning rejected audio targets; and same-track move overlap handling shifted a `2..7` move request to `10..15` via overlap resolution.

node --import tsx -e "import React from 'react'; import { act } from 'react'; import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'; import { JSDOM } from 'jsdom'; import { useExternalDrop } from './src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts'; import { planMultiDragMoves, applyMultiDragMoves } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/multi-drag-utils.ts'; const dom = new JSDOM('
'); globalThis.window = dom.window; globalThis.document = dom.window.document; globalThis.HTMLElement = dom.window.HTMLElement; globalThis.navigator = dom.window.navigator; let handlers; const applyCalls = []; const dataRef = { current: { tracks: [{ id: 'A1', kind: 'audio', label: 'A1' }, { id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'V1' }], rows: [{ id: 'A1', actions: [] }, { id: 'V1', actions: [{ id: 'base', start: 0, end: 10, effectId: 'effect-base' }, { id: 'move', start: 12, end: 17, effectId: 'effect-move' }] }], meta: { base: { track: 'V1', clipType: 'hold', hold: 10 }, move: { track: 'V1', clipType: 'effect-layer', hold: 5, continuous: { type: 'glitch', intensity: 0.5 } } }, clipOrder: { A1: [], V1: ['base', 'move'] }, registry: { assets: {} } } }; const coordinator = { editAreaRef: { current: null }, lastPosition: { isNewTrack: false, trackId: 'A1', time: 2 }, update: () => ({ isNewTrack: false, trackId: 'A1', time: 2 }), end: () => {} }; const Test = () => { handlers = useExternalDrop({ dataRef, scale: 1, scaleWidth: 100, selectedTrackId: null, applyTimelineEdit: (...args) => applyCalls.push(args), patchRegistry: () => {}, registerAsset: () => {}, uploadAsset: async () => { throw new Error('unused'); }, invalidateAssetRegistry: async () => {}, resolveAssetUrl: async (file) => file, coordinator, registerGenerationAsset: () => null, uploadImageGeneration: async () => { throw new Error('unused'); }, handleAssetDrop: () => {}, handleAddTextAt: undefined }); return null; }; const root = createRoot(document.getElementById('root')); await act(async () => { root.render(React.createElement(Test)); }); const event = { preventDefault() {}, currentTarget: { dataset: {} }, dataTransfer: { types: ['effect-layer'], files: [], getData: () => '' }, clientX: 0, clientY: 0 }; await act(async () => { await handlers.onTimelineDrop(event); }); const [rows, metaUpdates, _removed, clipOrder] = applyCalls[0]; const dropped = rows.find((row) => row.id === 'V1')?.actions.find((action) => action.id !== 'base' && action.id !== 'move'); const plannedInvalidMove = planMultiDragMoves(dataRef.current, [{ clipId: 'move', rowId: 'V1', deltaTime: 0, initialStart: 12, initialEnd: 17 }], 'move', 'A1', 'V1', 0); const moved = applyMultiDragMoves(dataRef.current, [{ clipId: 'move', sourceRowId: 'V1', targetRowId: 'V1', newStart: 2 }]); const movedAction = moved.nextRows.find((row) => row.id === 'V1')?.actions.find((action) => action.id === 'move'); console.log(JSON.stringify({ droppedTrack: dropped ? metaUpdates[dropped.id].track : null, droppedType: dropped ? metaUpdates[dropped.id].clipType : null, droppedStart: dropped?.start ?? null, droppedEnd: dropped?.end ?? null, clipOrder: clipOrder.V1, invalidCrossKindMoveAllowed: plannedInvalidMove.canMove, overlapResolvedStart: movedAction?.start ?? null, overlapResolvedEnd: movedAction?.end ?? null, overlapResolvedTrack: moved.metaUpdates.move?.track ?? 'V1' })); await act(async () => { root.unmount(); });"

node --import tsx -e "import { buildTimelineData, rowsToConfig } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts'; import { serializeForDisk, validateSerializedConfig } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/serialize.ts'; import { splitClipAtPlayhead } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/editor-utils.ts'; import { createEffectLayerClipMeta } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts'; const inputConfig = { output: { resolution: '1920x1080', fps: 30, file: 'out.mp4' }, tracks: [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'V1' }], clips: [{ id: 'fx1', at: 2, track: 'V1', clipType: 'effect-layer', hold: 5, continuous: { type: 'glitch', intensity: 0.5, params: { amount: 2 } } }] }; validateSerializedConfig(inputConfig); const built = await buildTimelineData(inputConfig, { assets: {} }); const reserialized = serializeForDisk(built.resolvedConfig); const splitSource = { output: { resolution: '1920x1080', fps: 30, file: 'out.mp4' }, tracks: [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'V1' }], clips: [{ id: 'fx-split', at: 0, track: 'V1', clipType: 'effect-layer', hold: 10, continuous: { type: 'glitch', intensity: 0.7, params: { amount: 4 } } }] }; const splitBuilt = await buildTimelineData(splitSource, { assets: {} }); const splitResult = splitClipAtPlayhead(splitBuilt.resolvedConfig, 'fx-split', 4); const [leftClip, rightClip] = splitResult.config.clips; const resizedConfig = rowsToConfig([{ id: 'V1', actions: [{ id: 'fx-resize', start: 3, end: 11, effectId: 'effect-fx-resize' }] }], { 'fx-resize': createEffectLayerClipMeta('V1') }, { resolution: '1920x1080', fps: 30, file: 'out.mp4' }, { V1: ['fx-resize'] }, [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'V1' }]); console.log(JSON.stringify({ roundTripClip: reserialized.clips[0], builtMeta: built.meta.fx1, split: { leftHold: leftClip.hold, rightHold: rightClip.hold, leftContinuous: leftClip.continuous, rightContinuous: rightClip.continuous, nextSelectedClipId: splitResult.nextSelectedClipId }, resizedHold: resizedConfig.clips[0]?.hold ?? null }));"

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: ClipType now includes 'effect-layer'. 'npm run build' completed successfully, which ran TypeScript compilation before the Vite build.

Confirmed with a `node --import tsx` probe that an effect-layer clip with `hold: 5` returns `sourceDuration: 5` and `durationInFrames: 150` at 30fps, and `rowsToConfig()` preserves `hold` without media timing fields.

Confirmed `lookupEffect()` resolves both built-in continuous effects and a dynamically registered custom effect. `EffectLayerSequence` also short-circuits to raw children when `clip.continuous` is undefined or the effect is missing.
Added `if (clip.clipType === 'effect-layer') return null;` to `VisualClip`, and `npm run build` passed.
Verified `createEffectLayerClipMeta()` produces a serializer-safe clip meta object with `track`, `clipType: 'effect-layer'`, and `hold: 5` via the `node --import tsx` probe.
Verified in code that the footer shows a violet draggable control with the `Layers` icon and that `onDragStart` sets `effect-layer` on `dataTransfer`; the repository build passed after the change.
Verified `ClipAction` now renders effect-layer clips with a `Layers` icon, violet selected/unselected styles, and `clipMeta.continuous?.type || 'Effect Layer'` as the label.

Verified `ClipPanel` returns only `['effects', 'timing']` for effect-layer clips, renders only the continuous effect controls, and swaps timing inputs to a hold-duration field instead of speed/from/to.
Verified `OverlayEditor` now skips `effect-layer` clips before overlay view-model creation, so no preview move/crop/resize handles can be rendered for them.

Verified structurally that `TimelineRenderer` now separates effect-layer clips, iterates visual tracks bottom-to-top, and wraps previously accumulated lower-track content with `EffectLayerSequence` from higher tracks. This matches the intended time-bounded effect application and track-order composition model, and the full build passed.

Confirmed with a jsdom hook probe that an `effect-layer` drop produces a clip on a visual track and routes through `resolveOverlaps()`: a drop at `2s` onto an occupied `V1` lane created an `effect-layer` clip shifted to `10s..15s` with clip order updated to include the new clip.

Confirmed that effect-layer clips cannot land on audio tracks: a jsdom hook probe dropped an effect-layer onto `A1` and the handler created it on visual track `V1` instead, while cross-kind drag planning returned `canMove: false` for `V1 -> A1`.

Confirmed save/load persistence with a round-trip probe: `validateSerializedConfig()` accepted an effect-layer clip without `asset`, `buildTimelineData()` rebuilt it successfully, and `serializeForDisk()` preserved `clipType: 'effect-layer'`, `hold: 5`, and the full `continuous` settings.

Confirmed effect-layer operations by probe: splitting `hold: 10` at `4s` produced `hold: 4` and `hold: 6` clips that both kept the same `continuous` config, resizing `3..11` serialized to `hold: 8`, cross-kind moves were rejected, and a same-track overlap move request was shifted from `2..7` to `10..15`.
